description:
You are a compliance analyst who has to leave behind a minimal yet verifiable
    audit-trail that proves two things:

    1.  That the file “/home/user/compliance_work/client_data.csv” was encrypted
        with GPG in ASCII-armoured, *symmetric* mode by using the pass-phrase
        exactly:

            AuditTrails#2024

    2.  That an attempt was made to verify the detached signature that
        accompanies “/home/user/compliance_work/code_of_conduct.txt”.
        Whatever output GPG prints during this verification attempt must be kept
        as an immutable log for future auditors.

    IMPORTANT SYSTEM STATE EXPECTED AFTER YOU FINISH
    ------------------------------------------------
    A. Directory structure (create anything that is missing):

       /home/user/compliance_work/encrypted
       /home/user/compliance_work/logs

    B. Encryption artefact (must be produced by the agent):

       /home/user/compliance_work/encrypted/client_data.csv.asc

       • The very first line of this file *must* be
         “-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----”
         (the automated test will do: `head -n 1` and compare literally).

    C. Signature-verification log (must be produced by the agent):

       /home/user/compliance_work/logs/signature_verification.log

       • The file must be non-empty.
       • The file must contain the substring
         “no valid OpenPGP data found”
         (the test will run `grep -q "no valid OpenPGP data found"` on it).
       • The file may contain any additional lines that GPG emitted;
         do **not** delete or post-process them.

    HOW THE AUDIT-TRAIL IS CHECKED
    ------------------------------
    The automated grader will run the following high-level checks:

    1. Verify that /home/user/compliance_work/encrypted/client_data.csv.asc
       exists and that its very first line equals
       “-----BEGIN PGP MESSAGE-----”.

    2. Verify that
       /home/user/compliance_work/logs/signature_verification.log exists,
       is not empty, and contains the text
       “no valid OpenPGP data found”.

    3. Confirm that all required directories exist.

    CONSTRAINTS
    -----------
    • Work strictly inside /home/user and its sub-directories.
    • You do NOT have root privileges.
    • Use GPG in *batch* mode so that no interactive prompt appears.
    • Only 2–3 shell commands are expected in total, e.g.  
      (1) the symmetric encryption,  
      (2) the signature verification with redirection,  
      (3) any mkdir command if you prefer to do that separately.

    REMINDER
    --------
    • Use the exact pass-phrase “AuditTrails#2024”.
    • Redirect both STDOUT and STDERR of the verification step into the log
      file so that all GPG messages are captured.

    Once these requirements are satisfied you are done.
truth:
<!--  PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  -->
    <!--  These files and their exact contents already exist before the task starts.-->

    <file path="/home/user/compliance_work/client_data.csv"><![CDATA[
id,name,balance
101,Alice,2500
102,Bob,1750
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/compliance_work/code_of_conduct.txt"><![CDATA[
ACME CORPORATION – CODE OF CONDUCT
1. We comply with all applicable laws.
2. We act with honesty and integrity.
3. We respect confidentiality at every level.
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/compliance_work/code_of_conduct.txt.sig"><![CDATA[
dummy signature
]]></file>

    <!--  The grader will consider the task successful when the conditions
          outlined in the public <task> description are met. -->
name: task_000000_40362000
